title: alyssum repens
intro: Alyssum repens, also known as creeping alyssum, is a low-growing, flowering perennial plant that belongs to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. Alyssum repens is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. Alyssum repens can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
